narrative: WTI crude surges toward $70 on reports of Iranian strikes on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, introducing a geopolitical risk premium that supports the dollar. USD/JPY trades at 161.89 as the 275bp Fed-BoJ nominal carry advantage offsets softer Japanese wage data, while the options market maintains a moderate put skew with USD/JPY 25d RR at -1.43. Gold consolidates lower -0.26%; equities firm +0.72%; DXY consolidates -0.01%; VIX eases 15.6. Watch the RBNZ monetary policy decision tomorrow at 02:00 UTC.
